node.name: Baked Salmon Plate — Mild-Spice Skillet
node.short_description: Baked salmon with mild spices, lemon, mixed vegetables, and rice.
node.meal_suggestion: Serving idea: 4–6 oz salmon, 1 cup cooked rice, 1–2 cups mixed vegetables; drizzle with lemon juice and a touch of olive oil or herbs. For more fiber, swap white rice for brown rice or quinoa.
node.nutrition_note: High-protein, omega-3-rich salmon; moderate carbs from rice; fiber and micronutrients from mixed vegetables; lemon adds vitamin C. Overall balanced and heart-healthy; suitable for portion-controlled dinners. Use minimal added salt if using spice blends.

matched_nodes.tree_path: Root > MealPlan_Version 1 > Child > Day 7 > Dinner > Baked Salmon Plate — Maple-Kissed Bake

node.name: Baked Salmon Plate — Maple-Kissed Bake
node.short_description: Baked salmon with lemon, served with mixed vegetables and rice.
node.meal_suggestion: Serve salmon with a squeeze of lemon over a bed of rice and accompany with steamed or roasted mixed vegetables for a balanced plate.
node.nutrition_note: High-protein meal with omega-3 fats from salmon; complex carbohydrates from rice; fiber and micronutrients from mixed vegetables; vitamin C from lemon. Low in added sugars unless a glaze is used.

matched_nodes.tree_path: Root > MealPlan_Version 1 > Child > Day 7 > Dinner > Chicken Fajita Bowl — Sesame Wrap

node.name: Chicken Fajita Bowl — Sesame Wrap
node.short_description: A protein-forward fajita bowl with chicken, peppers, onion, rice, and avocado, finished with sesame-inspired flavor.
node.meal_suggestion: For more fiber or lower glycemic load, swap white rice for brown rice or cauliflower rice, and keep avocado. If making a wrap, use a sesame wrap with the same fillings and consider adding salsa or lime for brightness.
node.nutrition_note: Protein-rich chicken with peppers and onion provides fiber and micronutrients; rice supplies energy with carbohydrates; avocado adds heart-healthy fats and satiety. Sesame wrap variation can add extra fats and flavor. Salt content depends on seasoning.

matched_nodes.tree_path: Root > MealPlan_Version 1 > Child > Day 7 > Dinner > Homemade Pizza Night — Mild-Spice Wrap

node.name: Homemade Pizza Night — Mild-Spice Wrap
node.short_description: Mild pizza-inspired wrap with whole-wheat crust, tomato sauce, mozzarella, mushrooms, and bell pepper — a fiber-rich, veggie-forward dish with calcium and protein.
node.meal_suggestion: Serve as a 1-wrap portion; pair with a large green salad and water to create a balanced meal.
node.nutrition_note: Fiber from whole-wheat crust supports fullness; vegetables add vitamins, while mozzarella provides protein and calcium. Monitor sodium and saturated fat from cheese and sauce; for a lighter option, use part-skim mozzarella and a bit less cheese, and pair with a side salad to boost fiber.

matched_nodes.tree_path: Root > MealPlan_Version 1 > Child > Day 6 > Dinner > Stuffed Bell Peppers — Citrus Fresh Wrap

node.name: Stuffed Bell Peppers — Citrus Fresh Wrap
node.short_description: Stuffed bell peppers filled with lean ground turkey, rice, and tomato sauce, topped with cheddar for a savory, protein-rich dish.
node.meal_suggestion: Serve with a bright citrus side salad (greens with orange or lemon vinaigrette) to fit the Citrus Fresh Wrap theme, and consider swapping in brown rice and reduced-fat cheese for extra fiber and lower fat.
node.nutrition_note: Lean ground turkey provides protein; peppers and rice contribute fiber and carbohydrates; cheddar adds calcium and fat; tomato sauce supplies lycopene. Overall a balanced meal with protein, fiber, and modest fat. For a lighter version, use lean turkey, brown rice, and low-sodium sauce.
